pi_items.metadata_value: computed at runtime (approx 150-300 based on script comments)

privileged_info_summary: The Agatston scoring system uses a standard Hounsfield unit (HU) threshold of 130 to identify calcified lesions. Density weighting factors are applied based on the peak HU of the lesion: 130-199 (factor 1), 200-299 (factor 2), 300-399 (factor 3), and 400 or greater (factor 4). Clinical risk categories for the total Agatston score are classified as: 0 (None), 1-10 (Minimal), 11-100 (Mild), 101-400 (Moderate), and >400 (Severe).
